summary: Tested new Featured Projects (homepage bento), /projects page with tag filters, ProjectModal, and full Admin CMS auth+CRUD. Backend: 18/19 pytest cases passed (94.7%). Frontend: all critical flows passed via Playwright — homepage featured tiles, modal, View All button, navbar (desktop+mobile) Projects link, /projects filtering+grid+modal, admin login (wrong/correct creds), protected route redirect, dashboard stats, Add/Edit/Delete project (with TagInput suggestion+custom tag), logout+re-redirect. One backend security bug found: brute-force lockout does not trigger.

minor.issue: Brute-force lockout (5 attempts/15min) never triggers 429. RCA: identifier is built as f'{request.client.host}:{email}' in auth.py check_brute_force/record_failed_login. In this environment, request.client.host alternates between two different upstream/proxy IPs (observed 10.169.1.66 and 10.169.2.130) across sequential requests to the same endpoint, splitting the failed-attempt counter across two separate 'identifier' documents in login_attempts collection. Neither ever reaches 5, so the account is never locked. Verified via direct curl (6 sequential wrong-password POSTs all returned 401, never 429) and by inspecting db.login_attempts which shows 2 docs with count=3 each for the same email instead of one doc with count=6. Fix: key the lockout by email only (safer for a single-admin CMS) or use a trusted X-Forwarded-For header behind the actual client-facing proxy instead of request.client.host.

ui_bugs.issue: Radix a11y console warning: 'DialogContent requires a DialogTitle for the component to be accessible for screen reader users.' Modal visually shows a title but Radix doesn't detect it as the accessible DialogTitle element (or it's not linked via aria). Cosmetic/accessibility only, no functional impact observed.

action_items: Fix brute-force lockout identifier logic in auth.py (see RCA) — currently non-functional in this proxied environment, meaning unlimited login attempts are effectively possible against the admin account.
Optional: wrap ProjectModal's title with proper Radix DialogTitle linkage to remove a11y console warning.

Main agent should actually populate /app/memory/test_credentials.md with admin credentials as claimed in the review request — file was found empty during testing (had to source credentials from the review request/backend .env instead).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py/auth.py: MongoDB _id properly excluded via Project.from_mongo()/BaseDocument pattern — good practice, no raw ObjectId leakage observed in any API response.
All /api/projects mutation endpoints correctly protected with Depends(get_current_admin) — verified 401 on unauthenticated POST/PUT/DELETE.
bcrypt hash format confirmed as $2b$ — correct.
httpOnly, samesite=lax cookies set correctly on login; CORS configured with allow_credentials=True and explicit origin (not '*') — correct for cookie-based auth.
seed_admin() correctly updates password hash if ADMIN_PASSWORD env changes and existing hash doesn't match — verified admin login worked with credentials from .env/review request.

Brute force lockout logic itself (record_failed_login/check_brute_force) is correctly written in isolation, but the identifier construction using request.client.host is fragile in any environment with multiple upstream/proxy hops — this is the root cause of the failure, not a logic bug in the counting itself.
test_credentials: admin@altioslabs.com / Altios@Admin2026 (sourced from review request + backend/.env; NOT found in /app/memory/test_credentials.md which was empty)
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_-prefixed project via pytest (test_create_update_delete_project_lifecycle) and via Playwright UI flow (TEST_UI_Project / TEST_UI_Project_Edited) — both fully deleted after test, no residual test data left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Projects/Admin CMS feature is functionally solid end-to-end (public pages, modal, filters, admin auth, CRUD, logout, route protection all verified working). The one real bug is the brute-force lockout never triggering due to request.client.host being unstable behind this environment's proxy — reproduce with 6 sequential curl POSTs to /api/auth/login with wrong password from the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and inspect db.login_attempts for split identifiers. All other auth/security checks (401 on unauth CRUD, cookie flags, bcrypt format, CORS) passed cleanly.
rca of the issue: See backend_issues.minor[0] issue field for full RCA of brute-force lockout bug.
